text:
Brazil’sRebeca Andradewon the women’s floor gold medal and Italy’sAlice D’Amatocaptured the beam title in the women’s event events, whileZou Jingyuanof China defended his title on parallel bars and Japan’sOka Shinnosukecollected his third gold medal in Paris by winning high bar in the last day of competitions at the Olympic Games.

In an error filled beam final Alice D’Amato won a surprise gold medal on balance beam, becoming the first Italian female gymnast to win an Olympic gold medal. D’Amato, who also won a silver with the team, but narrowly missed on a medal in the all-around and the uneven bars final, scored a 14.366 to top the field on beam for the historic gold.

“If I didn’t have any words after winning silver in the team competition, I definitely don’t have any words right now”, D’Amato said. “I didn’t even expect to win a medal on beam, let alone the gold…I tried to keep concentrated on my routine. I was very disappointed after having narrowly missed out on the medals in the all-around and the uneven bars final. I really didn’t want to go home without another medal. So, I persevered to achieve this medal”.

China’sZhou Yaqinearned the silver medal despite grabbing the beam after a combination of side somie to full twisting tuck jump to full twisting straight jump. Zhou, the 2023 World silver medalist and top qualifier to the final scored 14.100 to take the silver, the second medal for the Chinese team in Paris.

By taking the bronze medal with a score 14.000Manila Esposito, along with D’Amato made history as the first Italian women to win an Olympic medal on balance beam.

Due to a fall on her series and a .3 neutral deduction for not saluting the judges properly after the routine, already three-time gold medalist in ParisSimone Bilesof the U.S. received a 13.100 and placed fifth.

The first final for the men on the last day of competition was parallel bars where Zou Jingyuan easily defended his title with a score of 16.20. By winning the gold medal, Zou earned his third medal in Paris (silver with the team and on rings).

“Today, I wanted to perfect my landing, which I didn’t achieve yesterday (in the rings final), and today I did. The joy I felt wasn’t just about winning the gold medal; it was my satisfaction with my gymnastics”, Zou said.

Illia Kovtunof Ukraine deservingly won the silver, with a well-executed routine that received 15.500. The silver medal is the first and only medal for Ukraine in gymnastics at this Olympics, and the first ever Olympic medal for Kovtun.

Oka Shinnosukeof Japan added to his collection of team and all-around gold medals a bronze by placing third behind Kovtun with 15.300.



In the women’s floor final Rebeca Andrade of Brazil narrowly outscored gold medal favorite Simone Biles 14.166 to 14.133 to take her fourth medal in Paris and first gold medal of the competition.

“I knew about my capabilities of winning medals, and I gave it my all. I was fighting to finally have a gold, because I wanted that one so badly. I’m so glad I managed today”, Andrade said.

Two messy landings cost Biles the gold medal as she incurred .3 neutral deductions on two of her tumbling passes for going out of bound.

“It’s been up-and-down emotions. I couldn’t be more proud of the performances that I put on. It’s been a long week, competing several times, so I’m not mad about how I performed. It’s a lot of work and it’s paid off. I’m excited I was able to compete with some of the best in the world”, Biles said. This is the first time Biles has been defeated at the world stage (Worlds or Olympics) in a floor final.

Despite a subpar performance in the last day of competition, Biles will leave Paris with three gold medals and one silver, and with the overall result she surpassesShannon Milleras the most decorated U.S. Olympic gymnast of all time with 11 Olympic medals, 7 of them gold. Miller who competed at the 1992 and 1996 Games has a total of 7 medals. Tied with Vera Caslavska of Czechoslovakia Biles has the second most Olympic medals in history with 11, only behind the legendary Larisa Latynina of the Soviet Union who has 18.

After an inquiryJordan Chilesof the U.S. earned the floor bronze medal with a score of 13.766. This is Chiles’ first individual Olympic medal.

The competition didn’t end without a controversy. Romania’sSabrina Maneca-Voinea, who finished fifth in the floor final with 13.700 was penalized for stepping out of bound; however, the deduction was questionable as it didn’t appear that her heel had ever touched the floor area outside of the boundaries.  In addition, an inquiry for her routine was submitted for an approval for value credit on a jump, but the inquiry was denied.

In the last final for the men, high bar, six of the finalists made major errors in their routines. Olympic all-around champion took advantage of that and with a smooth and clean routine scored 14.533 to take the gold medal.

“I didn’t really expect to win a gold medal on the horizontal bar. I didn’t make any mistakes today and I am very happy with my routine.” Oka was the most decorated male gymnast at this Olympics with three gold medals and one bronze (team, all-around, high bar gold, and parallel bars bronze).

“Yeah, after the team final the pressure was off my shoulders. I was just able to perform with less pressure. I’m happy I was able to do so well”, he said about his successful performances.

Colombia’sAngel Barajaswon a historic silver for his country by posting the same score as Oka on high bar but losing the tie-breaker determined by the higher execution score. Despite fallsTang Chia-Hungof Chinese Taipei andZhang Bohengof China shared the bronze medal with a score 13.966.

In the men’s competition Japan leaves Paris as the most successful country having won the team gold medal and three gold medals and one bronze overall, followed closely by China winning two gold medals three silver and two bronze.

On the women’s side the United States dominated the field by winning three gold medals, one silver and four bronze medals.

Vanesa Mašová of Czechia teamed with U.S. gymnast David Shamah to capture the mixed-pairs Sokol Grand Prix, one of two competitions held in memory of legendary Czech gymnast Vera Caslavska this weekend in Brno, Czechia.

After notching perfect scores in two of their three performances, Olympic gold medalist Jordan Chiles and professional dance partner Ezra Sosa earned third place in ABC’s “Dancing with the Stars” season finale on Tuesday.

Double victor Camilo Vera of Colombia, Xiang Yina of China, Nishiyama Misa of Japan, Ojima Nao of Japan and AIN Arsenii Dukhno captured titles on the second of two days of apparatus finals at the Junior World Championships in Manila on Monday.

China and the U.S. each claimed two golds, and an AIN gymnast took the fifth title, on the first of two days of apparatus finals at the Junior World Championships in Manila on Sunday.
